name: Valletta
description: Malta's Baroque capital and UNESCO World Heritage city. Explore the Grand Harbour, St John's Co-Cathedral, and the Upper Barrakka Gardens.

name: Mdina
description: The Silent City — a medieval walled town perched on a hilltop. Best visited early morning before the crowds. Stunning views across the island.

name: Gozo Island
description: Malta's quieter sister island. Take the 25-minute ferry from Ċirkewwa. Visit the Citadella, Azure Window site, and swim at Ramla Bay.

name: Marsaxlokk
description: A traditional fishing village famous for its colourful luzzu boats. Sunday market is a highlight — fresh fish, local produce, and crafts.

name: Blue Lagoon
description: Malta's most famous swimming spot on Comino island. Crystal-clear turquoise water. Take a ferry from Ċirkewwa or St Paul's Bay.

name: Sliema & St Julian's
description: Malta's modern seafront strip. Great for restaurants, cafés, and evening walks along the promenade. Vibrant nightlife in Paceville.
